subject: SCIENCE
title: Homes That Meet Needs
introduction: A suitable environment provides the particular resources and conditions an organism needs. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Homes That Meet Needs”.
A key word used in the lesson “Homes That Meet Needs”.
A key word used in the lesson “Homes That Meet Needs”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: A suitable environment provides the particular resources and conditions an organism needs.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: A suitable environment provides the particular resources and conditions an organism needs.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

doesNotFit: Habitat is only a sleeping place

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: Why might a pond suit a tadpole?

options: It provides water and resources it needs.

feedback.correct: Correct: It provides water and resources it needs.
feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. It is made of paper. and All animals must live there. do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Homes That Meet Needs”?

options.text: A suitable environment provides the particular resources and conditions an organism needs.
Habitat is only a sleeping place

items.explanation: The correct response is “A suitable environment provides the particular resources and conditions an organism needs.” because it follows the lesson explanation: A suitable environment provides the particular resources and conditions an organism needs.

options.text: Habitat is only a sleeping place

items.explanation: The correct response is “pond for tadpole” because it follows the lesson explanation: A suitable environment provides the particular resources and conditions an organism needs.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “One home suits every organism” because it follows the lesson explanation: A suitable environment provides the particular resources and conditions an organism needs.

items.prompt: Why might a pond suit a tadpole?

options.text: It provides water and resources it needs.

items.explanation: The correct response is “It provides water and resources it needs.” because it follows the lesson explanation: A suitable environment provides the particular resources and conditions an organism needs.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?
items.explanation: The correct response is “soil for earthworm” because it follows the lesson explanation: A suitable environment provides the particular resources and conditions an organism needs.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Homes That Meet Needs; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Homes That Meet Needs.

safetyNotes: Use the on-screen activity only; no unsupervised physical experiment is required.
